title: Crotalaria hamiltonii
uses: Crotalaria hamiltonii has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, it is believed to have antioxidant properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall health. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Crotalaria hamiltonii for medicinal use.
taxonomy: Crotalaria hamiltonii is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. The genus Crotalaria is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species C. hamiltonii is further classified as a member of the subgenus Crotalaria. The classification of C. hamiltonii is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.

The plant's extracts have also been found to exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals. side_effects: Crotalaria hamiltonii contains compounds that may cause side effects due to its toxic properties. It contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which are toxic to humans and animals. Ingestion of Crotalaria hamiltonii can lead to liver damage and cirrhosis. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. These side effects are a result of the plant's toxic properties and can be severe if not handled properly.
